svg.aria-label: One host multiplexing two MCP servers into a single run-time tool palette, contrasted with the old hardcoded single-process tool dict. On the left, the OLD way: a hardcoded TOOL_SCHEMAS dict baked into one process, holding search_policy and process_refund. It cannot be shared, swapped, or discovered by another host. In the center, the NEW way: a single HOST connects over JSON-RPC to two separate servers. The support-server exposes tools search_policy and process_refund, a resource file:///policies/refund.md, and a prompt refund_decision. The catalog-server exposes the tool search_products. Each connection runs the initialize handshake at protocolVersion 2025-06-18 and a tools/list discovery. On the right, the host merges the discovered tools into a single run-time PALETTE the controller draws from: search_policy from support-server, process_refund from support-server, and search_products from catalog-server. A multi-hop task uses search_products from catalog and process_refund from support transparently off the same palette. The point is that the action space is assembled at run time from whatever servers the host connects to, not hardcoded at author time.
svg.title: One host multiplexes two MCP servers into a single run-time palette, replacing the hardcoded single-process tool dict

svg.text: Tools stop being a dict baked into one process and become discovered, standardized capabilities. Any host can use any server; the agent's palette is a run-time union, not an author-time constant.
